items.briefing:
Australian healthcare organisations are investing more in cybersecurity, but many lack confidence in their ability to recover from an attack, according to a recent Wasabi survey. Separately, accounts payable teams are being warned about rising fraud risks as AI makes it easier for criminals to impersonate suppliers or manipulate payment processes. Staff handling invoices or financial approvals should be especially cautious about unexpected payment requests.

There is also growing discussion around a "digital duty of care," putting pressure on Australian organisations to be more accountable for how AI tools are used responsibly and securely.

**Practical tip:** Always verify unexpected payment requests or changes to supplier bank details by calling the supplier directly on a known number — not one included in the email.

featured.title: Mackay Sugar cyber attack flagged as broader risk to Australia’s food supply chain
featured.quote: Critical infrastructure vulnerability exposed as major Australian food exporter targeted by cyber attack.
